behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: manifest parser/resolver must pass a complete dependency set to the vulnerability-check stage. A unit test on only the extractor can miss real failures where transitive deps are dropped between modules. Higher levels are not required because the behavior text is about local extraction/inclusion, not real HTTP/API behavior or AI output.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner pipeline in-process with fixture manifests/lockfiles containing direct and transitive deps; assert the dependency set sent to the vuln-query module includes both, with no omissions/duplicates.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real external API query semantics for version ranges. A mocked client can always return correctly filtered results and hide failures where the real service ignores/misinterprets range constraints. Lower-level miss proof: Integration with stubs would miss production failures caused by real HTTP parameter encoding or backend range parsing.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real vulnerability database endpoint (or official sandbox), issue queries derived from manifest ranges and assert returned CVEs correspond to the exact constrained versions, not package-name-only matches.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: Pure deterministic ordering logic within one function/object boundary; no cross-module, infra, or model dependency is required to reveal the real failure.
behaviors.test_description: Provide unsorted CVE fixtures with mixed severities and CVSS ties; assert exact output order by severity rank, then descending CVSS within each severity.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Real failure typically appears across parse-edit-serialize modules: updating one dependency can accidentally rewrite unrelated entries. A unit test of only the version-selection function can miss serializer-side mutations. Rejected System because behavior text is local manifest modification (no OS/network/subprocess requirement beyond local file/module wiring).
behaviors.test_description: Use real manifest fixtures through the full local update path (parse + mutate + write/render) and diff before/after to assert exactly one dependency version change and byte-for-byte stability of unaffected entries where format preservation is expected.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires creation on a real repository, crossing external API/network boundaries. Mocks can hide auth, branch, permission, and diff-application failures that only occur against the real host.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner against a real test repo/branch and verify an actual PR is opened, then validate PR files changed contain exactly the one intended dependency bump.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Deterministic semver comparison logic at a single function boundary. No module wiring or external infra is required to expose the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Feed semver pairs covering major/minor/patch/prerelease/build cases and assert the breaking-change classifier returns true only for major-version-increase scenarios per chosen semver rules.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state handoff across local modules (semver detector -> decision logic -> PR client/output formatter). Unit tests of one component can miss the real failure where detection does not propagate to gating/output. Rejected System because behavior text only requires skipping PR creation and surfacing a flag, which is verifiable in-process with a fake PR client; no real network/API needed for this behavior itself.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end local scanner flow with input that triggers breaking-change=true; assert PR module is not invoked and scanner output contains the review-required flag with the expected dependency/context.
